one_time_description: Atikameksheng Trust 21st anniversary deemed disposition resulting in $924,853 disbursement to First Nation, which was then contributed back to the Trust as capital
economic_notes: First Nation holds 51% interest in Bagone'an JS Drilling Inc. and 51% interest in Eshkaa Niibiish-Day Inc. (not yet operational). Also holds units in Ontario First Nations Limited Partnership and Ontario First Nations Sovereign Wealth Limited Partnership. Business Park development, Solar Park, reserve road improvement and sewer main connection projects underway with FNFA financing up to $8,000,000.
notes: Fiscal year 2021 (ended March 31, 2021). Two fully consolidated subsidiaries: 1988183 Ontario Limited and Chi-Zhiingwaak Business Park Corporation. Two GBEs accounted for under modified equity method: Bagone'an JS Drilling Inc. (51% owned) and Eshkaa Niibiish-Day Inc. (51% owned, not yet operational). Long-term debt total is $1,340,771 (CMHC and TD mortgages) plus $3,665,745 (FNFA debt) = $5,006,516. Housing expenses split between Housing-Other ($69,699) and CMHC Housing ($266,737). osr calculated as total revenue $17,906,343 minus ISC funding $6,014,251 = $11,892,092 (note: other government transfers from Ontario ministries, CMHC, etc. are embedded in the 'Other' revenue line and cannot be fully separated). No schedule of remuneration provided in the extracted text. COVID-19 impacts noted.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
